question: Is the central bronze sculpture positioned noticeably closer to the camera (lower in the frame) compared to its original location?
Does the sculpture appear larger in scale, consistent with the perspective shift of moving approximately 6 feet closer to the viewer?
Is the sculpture rotated clockwise by approximately 15 degrees relative to its original orientation?
Has the reflection of the sculpture on the marble floor been correctly repositioned and rotated to match the object's new state?
Is the shadow cast by the sculpture shifted forward to correspond accurately with the sculpture's new position and the room's lighting?
Is the area where the sculpture originally stood seamlessly inpainted, revealing the continuous marble floor pattern without seams or ghosting artifacts?
Are any visitors or background elements previously hidden by the sculpture now visible and rendered with correct anatomy and proportions?
Does the sculpture retain its original bronze material texture and golden-brown color without unwanted hue shifts?
Are the large abstract paintings on the left and right walls preserved in their original positions and appearance?
Do the visitors standing on the sides of the gallery or sitting on benches remain in their original locations and poses?
Is the arched doorway at the far end of the gallery preserved intact?
Does the sculpture appear properly grounded on the floor (not floating or tilting) with perspective lines consistent with the room?
Does the shadow exhibit soft gradients consistent with the diffuse lighting from the skylight, rather than harsh cutout edges?
Are the edges of the moved sculpture seamlessly integrated into the new position without visible cut-and-paste artifacts or jagged lines?
Is the edited image free from unnatural blur, resolution loss, or noise, particularly in the inpainted regions and on the sculpture itself?
